summary: Frontend-only validation completed for client journey updates via static review plus unauthenticated runtime checks on web. Deep-links /explore and /appointments are protected by session and redirect to /login without 404. Build checks (TypeScript, expo export web, expo-doctor) passed.

integration_issues.issue: Legacy browser dialog usage remains in client flow file via window.alert wrapper, violating requirement to avoid window.alert/confirm/prompt.

Could not validate Explore data rendering, Appointments filtering by client_id, tab behavior, inline cancellation confirmation, and Barbershop profile real-data CTAs end-to-end because no test credentials/session were available.

action_items: Remove window.alert/confirm/prompt usage from client flow files (notably /(client)/booking) and use existing inline notice/toast pattern.
Provide authenticated client credentials (or deterministic non-production seed) so next agent can execute full runtime checks for Explore/Appointments/Barbershop Profile behavior.
Run authenticated regression specifically for appointments query scoping by client_id and inline cancellation path after credentials are available.
critical_code_review_comments: Required IndexedDB mitigation is implemented correctly: extraIncrementalIDBOptions.onversionchange exists in /src/database/index.web.ts.
Client deep-link protection is implemented in /src/app/_layout.tsx (explore and appointments recognized as client-group segments and redirected to login when unauthenticated).
Data-testid coverage is strong in new client experience components for critical interactive elements; however, authenticated behavior remains unverified due to missing credentials.
test_credentials: Read /app/memory/test_credentials.md. File states no existing test accounts; authenticated customer flow validation is pending.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Unauthenticated web checks passed for /explore and /appointments route availability (no 404) and session protection (redirect to /login). No horizontal overflow detected at 1920x1080 and 390x844 on tested unauthenticated screens. Build health checks passed: yarn tsc --noEmit, yarn build:web, npx expo-doctor (20/20).
rca of the issue: Reproduction for dialog violation: run text scan on /app/src and inspect /(client)/booking.tsx; wrapper still calls window.alert on web. This is leftover legacy feedback handling and conflicts with the no-browser-dialog requirement. Mitigation: replace web alert fallback with non-blocking in-app notice component already used in new experience screens.
